title: pyrus × arnoldiana
intro: Pyrus × arnoldiana, also known as the Arnold's pear or mountain pear, is a hybrid species of pear tree that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The health benefits of Pyrus × arnoldiana include its ability to reduce pain, fever, and inflammation, making it a useful remedy for treating various ailments such as headaches, colds, and sore throats. Herbal preparations of Pyrus × arnoldiana, including infusions and tinctures, can be made from its bark, leaves, and flowers, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
